requirements.text: WHEN a ticket has related sub-document files or folders, the system shall expose main plus discovered top-level entries for navigation.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Document discovery

requirements.text: WHEN no custom order is configured, the system shall order recognized top-level entries as requirements, domain, architecture, poc, tests, tasks, debt.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Default top-level subdocument order

requirements.text: WHEN .mdt-config.toml defines project.ticketSubdocuments, the system shall order matching top-level entries according to that configuration.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Configuration

requirements.text: WHEN discovered top-level entries are not named in the configured or default order, the system shall append them after ordered entries using natural ascending name order.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Ordering

requirements.text: WHILE a ticket has no related sub-document entries, the system shall omit sub-document navigation and display only the main ticket document.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Conditional tabs

requirements.text: WHEN sub-document navigation is shown, the system shall present top-level files and folders in the primary tab row.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Folder-backed tabs

requirements.text: WHEN a folder entry is selected, the system shall reveal that folder's children in the next tab row.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Nested folders

requirements.text: WHEN a nested folder entry is selected, the system shall reveal another tab row for the next folder level.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Nested folders

requirements.text: WHEN grouped folders such as prep/, poc/, or part-* are present, the system shall present them as grouped navigation entries rather than flattened descendant names.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Folder-backed tabs

requirements.text: WHILE a folder entry is selected, the system shall preserve the currently displayed document content until a descendant file entry is selected.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Nested folders

requirements.text: WHEN a user selects main or a file entry, the system shall load and display the corresponding markdown document.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Lazy loading

requirements.text: WHILE selected document content is loading, the system shall display loading feedback in the content area.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Lazy loading

requirements.text: WHILE the user scrolls ticket content, the system shall keep sub-document navigation visible.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Sticky positioning

requirements.text: WHILE sticky navigation is active, the system shall avoid layout shift that disrupts reading or navigation.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Non-Functional: No layout shift

requirements.text: WHEN a user selects a non-main document, the system shall update the URL hash to the selected relative document path.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: URL routing

requirements.text: WHEN a user selects a nested document, the system shall encode the nested path in the URL hash using slash-separated folder segments.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: URL routing

requirements.text: WHEN a page loads with a valid sub-document hash, the system shall reopen the required folder levels and display the targeted document.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Hash fallback

requirements.text: IF the URL hash references a document path that no longer exists, THEN the system shall fall back to main.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Hash fallback

requirements.text: WHEN the underlying sub-document structure changes, the system shall update the visible navigation to match the current files and folders.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Real-time updates

requirements.text: IF the active document is removed by a realtime update, THEN the system shall switch the visible document to main.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: Functional

requirements.text: IF a selected document fails to load, THEN the system shall display an error in the content area without removing available navigation.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Real-time updates

requirements.text: WHILE realtime update delivery is unavailable, the system shall continue to allow manual navigation using the last successfully loaded structure.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Non-Functional: Realtime Fallback

requirements.text: Sub-documents are discovered from ticket-related files and directories, not created synthetically in the UI.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 1 Technical Constraints

requirements.text: Ordering comes from backend discovery plus project configuration, not client-side resorting.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 1 Technical Constraints

requirements.text: Sub-document navigation uses shadcn Tabs.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 1 Technical Constraints

requirements.text: Deep links use stable relative document paths in the URL hash.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 1 Technical Constraints

requirements.text: Realtime delivery failure does not block manual navigation.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 1 Technical Constraints

requirements.text: Existing markdown rendering remains the content rendering pipeline for sub-documents.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 1 Technical Constraints

requirements.text: Tab switching begins content loading within 100ms.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Non-Functional

requirements.text: Sub-document parsing supports markdown files up to 1MB.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Non-Functional

requirements.text: Sticky navigation does not create disruptive layout shift.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Non-Functional

requirements.text: OpenAPI documentation is updated for sub-document API changes.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 1 Technical Constraints

requirements.text: WHEN the ticket detail API returns sub-document metadata, the system shall represent files and folders in a hierarchical structure sufficient to render ordered multi-row navigation.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Main ticket API

requirements.text: WHEN the individual sub-document retrieval API returns a document, the system shall include code, content, dateCreated, and lastModified.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: Sub-document retrieval API

requirements.text: WHEN sub-document API changes are delivered, the system shall publish matching OpenAPI documentation for those changes.
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4 Feature Contract: OpenAPI Verification

scenarios.title: Sub-document navigation hidden when no related files
scenarios.given: a ticket with no related sub-document files or folders
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see only the main ticket document without tab navigation

scenarios.title: Folder tab reveals child documents in next row
scenarios.given: a ticket with folder sub-documents and I am on the ticket page
scenarios.then: a second tab row appears showing that folder's children and the current document content is preserved

scenarios.title: Grouped folders preserve hierarchy without flattening
scenarios.given: a ticket with grouped folders like prep/ poc/ or part-*
scenarios.then: I see each folder as a grouped navigation entry in the primary row rather than flattened descendant names

scenarios.title: Selecting document tab loads and displays content
scenarios.given: a ticket with sub-documents and I am on the ticket page
scenarios.when: I select a document tab other than main
scenarios.then: I see loading feedback followed by the corresponding markdown content

scenarios.title: Navigation remains visible while scrolling content
scenarios.given: a ticket with sub-documents and I have selected a tab with long content
scenarios.when: I scroll down through the document content
scenarios.then: the tab navigation stays visible and no layout shift disrupts my reading

scenarios.title: URL hash updates when selecting sub-document
scenarios.given: a ticket with nested folder sub-documents and I am on the ticket page
scenarios.then: the URL hash updates to encode the full relative path using slash-separated segments

scenarios.title: Navigation updates when sub-document structure changes
scenarios.given: I am viewing a ticket with sub-documents and have a sub-document selected
scenarios.when: the underlying file structure changes removing my active document
scenarios.then: the navigation updates to match the current files and I am switched to the main tab

scenarios.title: Manual navigation available when realtime updates unavailable
scenarios.given: I am viewing a ticket with sub-documents and realtime updates are unavailable
scenarios.when: I use the tab navigation to select different documents
scenarios.then: I can still navigate using the last successfully loaded structure

scenarios.title: Error displayed when document fails to load
scenarios.given: I am viewing a ticket with sub-documents and have selected a tab
scenarios.when: the document content fails to load
scenarios.then: I see an error message in the content area while the navigation remains available

scenarios.title: Sub-document tabs appear in configured or default order
scenarios.given: a ticket with related sub-document files; ordering is either default (no config) or custom (project.ticketSubdocuments set in .mdt-config.toml)
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see tab navigation with main plus discovered top-level entries in the configured or default order (requirements, domain, architecture, poc, tests, tasks, debt when default)
scenarios.sourceRef: requirements.md BR-1.1–BR-1.3, BR-2.1; Scenario Outline: <ordering_mode> in {default | custom_config}

scenarios.title: Entries not in configured order are appended alphabetically
scenarios.given: a ticket with sub-document files where some names are not in the configured or default order list
scenarios.when: I view the ticket detail page
scenarios.then: I see the known ordered entries first, followed by the remaining entries in ascending alphabetical order

scenarios.title: Deep link opens targeted sub-document on page load
scenarios.given: a ticket with sub-documents including a nested folder
scenarios.when: I open the ticket page with a URL hash pointing to a valid nested sub-document path
scenarios.then: the required folder levels open and the targeted document is displayed

scenarios.title: Deep link with missing target falls back to main
scenarios.when: I open the ticket page with a URL hash referencing a sub-document that no longer exists
scenarios.then: the main tab is shown instead

obligations.title: Server owns sub-document discovery and ordering; frontend consumes as delivered
One shadcn Tabs row per active folder level; folders reveal children in next row without flattening
useTicketDocumentNavigation is the sole frontend authority for selected path and folder-stack transitions
File selection triggers lazy content load via dataLayer; loading/error states surface in content area
Tab navigation remains visible during scroll without disruptive layout shift
SSE updates reconcile tree structure; removed active document falls back to main; manual navigation continues if SSE unavailable
API returns hierarchical sub-document metadata with CR; individual retrieval returns code, content, dates
Sub-document API changes published to openapi.yaml
Test scaffolding stays separate from runtime; no test-only logic in production code
Hide tab navigation when no sub-documents exist; show only main ticket content

testPlans.title: Sub-document API endpoints: subdocuments list ordering, individual retrieval, OpenAPI compliance
useTicketDocumentNavigation: selected path, folder stack, URL hash sync, fallback to main
useTicketDocumentRealtime: SSE reconciliation, active-removed callback, degraded-mode safety
Sub-document navigation E2E: tabs, ordering, folder rows, sticky, deep link, SSE, error

tasks.title: Backend: OpenAPI documentation for sub-document endpoints
Walking Skeleton: Create SubDocument model and backend discovery
Frontend: TicketDocumentTabs component with navigation hook
Frontend: Content loading hook with sticky navigation
Frontend: Deep linking with URL hash persistence
Frontend: Error handling and E2E integration
